description: Contract-Level Perturbations: Create new perturbation operators that modify the contract (required_properties, forbidden_shortcuts) not just prompt_text. Add curriculum levels 6 and 7 that use contract perturbations to break benchmark saturation where breaking_point is always null. Document thefts 5.65-5.68 from CheckList, Contrast Sets, Counterfactual Data, and Dynabench.
steps: Create git branch feat/real-world-upgrades from current HEAD (feat/production-integration)

Create virtual_genesis/eval/perturbations/contract_perturbations.py with 6 operators following the clone_case pattern from taskcase_variants.py: property_addition (adds extra required_property the template response won't satisfy), property_removal (removes a required_property to test verification correctly passes), shortcut_injection (adds a forbidden_shortcut targeting template response style), contract_flip (swaps a required_property to forbidden_shortcuts and vice versa), contract_tightening_strict (makes ALL properties mandatory with no partial credit), counterfactual_contract (creates mirror case where success criteria are inverted). Each operator must: call clone_case, modify the contract fields (required_properties/forbidden_shortcuts), add appropriate tag and meta[perturbation_type].

Update build_curriculum_levels() in virtual_genesis/eval/perturbations/taskcase_variants.py to add Level 6 (contract_flip + property_addition on top of level 5) and Level 7 (counterfactual_contract + contract_tightening_strict - maximum contract pressure). Update build_curriculum_from_cases to use limit_per_case=8 default.

Create tests/test_contract_perturbations.py with 15+ tests: test each of the 6 operators returns TaskCase, has correct tag, has correct meta, modifies contract (not just prompt), test that property_addition adds a property not already present, test that property_removal results in fewer properties, test that contract_flip swaps between lists, test that shortcut_injection adds to forbidden_shortcuts, test counterfactual_contract inverts success criteria, test contract_tightening_strict makes all strict, test build_curriculum_levels now produces 8 levels (0-7), test that level 6 and 7 cases have contract perturbation tags.

Create Virtual_SIA_Legitimate_Thefts_RealWorld_AR.md documenting thefts 5.65-5.68 in Arabic template format (CheckList Ribeiro 2020, Contrast Sets Gardner 2020, Counterfactual Data Kaushik 2020, Dynabench Kiela 2021).
Create Virtual_SIA_Contract_Perturbation_Memo_AR.md in Arabic explaining the benchmark saturation problem and how contract perturbations solve it.
acceptance_criteria: All 6 contract perturbation operators exist and are importable from contract_perturbations module
build_curriculum_levels produces 8 levels (0-7) with levels 6-7 using contract perturbations
All 297 existing tests continue to pass
Contract perturbation memo exists in Arabic
verification: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -q (all tests pass including original 297)
findings: Resolved circular import between contract_perturbations.py and taskcase_variants.py by using lazy import inside build_curriculum_levels(). Updated 2 existing tests that hardcoded old level count (6->8) and old default limit (6->8). All 344 tests pass (297 original + 47 new).
